4116	## Knowledge Base — Documentation-rot watch (iter-238 addition)
4117	
4118	- **Route changes outran their prose on `tensorObj_assoc_iso` (iter-238) — NON-blocking but actively misleading.** Both reviewers flagged stale descriptions of the SUPERSEDED flatness route: Lean docstring `TensorObjSubstrate.lean:302–340` still describes `W_whisker*_of_flat` + sectionwise flatness and claims sorry-transitivity through `isLocallyInjective_whiskerLeft_of_W` (the body uses `_of_W`, axiom-clean); module header L43–45 lists wrong residuals + omits §5; §5 (L730) sits BEFORE §4 (L865). Blueprint `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` L1452–1456 note + the `lem:tensorobj_assoc_iso` title still claim `IsLocallyTrivial` hyps that were dropped. Fixes are prover/refactor (Lean comments) + blueprint-writer (prose) — outside the review agent's domain; tracked in session_238/recommendations.md. **Lesson:** after a 2-step route change (flatness→route-(d)→hyp-drop), schedule a same-/next-iter comment+prose cleanup or the next agent misreads the sorry-debt of a load-bearing decl.
4119	
4120	## Knowledge Base — Proof patterns (iter-239 addition)
4121	
4122	- **Rewrite the EQUATION, not pointwise, for CommRingCat-coerced `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` legs (iter-239, `FlatBaseChange.lean`, closed `tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule`).** When a leg is a `ModuleCat.Hom.hom` over a `CommRingCat` coercion, `∘ₗ` does NOT unify the `∘ₛₗ` pattern, so `LinearMap.comp_apply`/`comp_assoc`/`simp [LinearMap.coe_comp]` all no-fire on `(f ∘ₗ ↑e) x`. **Recipe:** state the triangle as a linear-map equation `restriction ∘ₗ e.toLinearMap = target`, get it via `congrArg ModuleCat.Hom.hom htri` + `rw [ModuleCat.hom_comp]`, then rewrite to the `∘ₗ e.symm` form via `(LinearEquiv.eq_comp_toLinearMap_symm _ _).mpr`, and finish with `IsLocalizedModule.of_linearEquiv_right (powers b) (toOpen …).hom e.symm`.
4123	- **`basicOpen 1 = ⊤` inside a dependent type (iter-239).** `PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one ▸ inferInstance` and `simpa only [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one]` do NOT rewrite `basicOpen 1 → ⊤` inside the dependent `tilde.toOpen M (basicOpen 1)` (rewrite-pattern-not-found / typeclass-stuck). Use `have h := inferInstance; rw [PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_one] at h; exact h`.
4124	- **`gammaPushforwardIsoAt` — open-indexed `e_{D(a)}` (iter-239, `FlatBaseChange.lean`, axiom-clean).** `gammaPushforwardIso`'s element-free route (b) generalizes verbatim from `op ⊤` to any `op U / op V`: `Γ((Spec φ)_* N, U) ≅ restrictScalars φ (Γ(N, (Spec φ)⁻¹ U))` for all `U`, because `modulesSpecToSheaf` restricts scalars to the base ring uniformly at the top open (the same `restrictScalarsComp'App ×2 + eqToIso(globalSectionsIso_hom_comp_specMap_appTop)` reconciliation applies at any `U`). This is blueprint movement (1).
4125	- **`sheafifyTensorUnitIso` statement shape (iter-239, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`, axiom-clean).** The sheafification-monoidal reconciliation brick `a(P⊗ₚQ) ≅ a((aP).val ⊗ₚ (aQ).val)` (`a = PresheafOfModules.sheafification (𝟙 𝒪_X)`) is built by the SAME whisker-the-unit technique as `tensorObj_assoc_iso` (`W_whisker{Left,Right}_of_W` + `isIso_sheafification_map_of_W`, flatness-free). GOTCHAS: statement MUST spell `PresheafOfModules.Monoidal.tensorObj (R := X.presheaf)` (NOT `MonoidalCategory.tensorObj`, else `(aP).val` fails `MonoidalCategoryStruct` synth); the left-whisker object must be given EXPLICITLY `(a.obj P).val ◁ η.app Q`; keep the `letI instMS` `Sheaf.val` bridge. It is the eventual RHS reconciliation `pullbackTensorIso` consumes.
4126	
4127	## Knowledge Base — Known Blockers (iter-239 addition)
4128	
4129	- **`IsInvertible.pullback` via sectionwise `extendScalars` is STRUCTURALLY IMPOSSIBLE — do NOT re-dispatch the recipe (iter-239, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** `Scheme.Modules.pullback f = SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom`, and the underlying `PresheafOfModules.pullback φ.hom`, are BOTH `(pushforward _).leftAdjoint` — abstract left adjoints with **NO sectionwise and NO stalkwise formula** in the pinned Mathlib (grep of `ModuleCat/{Presheaf,Sheaf}/` finds no `pullback_obj`/`pullbackObjIso`/`pullback…Monoidal`). The plan-agent recipe ("presheaf pullback is strong-monoidal sectionwise via `(extendScalars f).Monoidal`; assemble the tensorators `μ`") cannot typecheck — there is no sectionwise `(pullback φ).obj` to attach the tensorator to. `Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal` gives the comparison map `δ : f^*(A⊗B) ⟶ f^*A⊗f^*B` for free, but only at PRESHEAF level (no `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules …)`); proving `IsIso δ` reduces back to the same missing sectionwise identification. `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is an iso only under `F.Final` (open immersions), false for general `f`. **PIVOT:** (1) local-chart-finality via the axiom-clean `isIso_of_isIso_restrict` + local FINAL map `g = f.resLE U V` (how `IsLocallyTrivial.pullback` does general `f`) — first build the Mathlib-absent `pullbackObjUnitToUnit`/`pullbackComp`/`restrictFunctorIsoPullback` naturality cluster; OR (2) a FLAT-restricted `IsInvertible.pullback` (the RPF structure maps `π_T`/base-changes are flat). `sheafifyTensorUnitIso` (landed this iter) is the RHS bridge route (1) will consume for the tensor part. Run a mathlib-analogist + strategy-critic pass BEFORE committing prover effort.
4130	- **FlatBaseChange `hloc` / `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso`: the Module-R carrier wall is in its 4th recurrence — do NOT re-dispatch a verbatim `hloc` round (iter-239).** The unconditional brick reduces (via `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso_of_isLocalizedModule`) to `hloc(a) = IsLocalizedModule (powers a)` on the pushforward-side restriction `Γ(N,⊤)→Γ(N,D a)`. The discharge needs an `R`-action on the `R'`-side sections via `Module.compHom _ φ.hom`, but `letI` it is NOT consumed by `LinearMap.restrictScalars R` (clashes with the `modulesSpecToSheaf`-supplied `R`-action); and the naturality square `e₂∘ρ = (restrictScalars φ σ)∘e₁` needs Mathlib-absent naturality of `restrictScalarsComp'App` in its module arg. **Two new axiom-clean bricks landed** (`gammaPushforwardIsoAt`, `tildeRestriction_isLocalizedModule`) — KEEP them and the `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` skeleton. **Next attempt MUST be one of:** (a) prove naturality of `gammaPushforwardIsoAt` in its open argument → `of_linearEquiv` closes `hloc`; (b) the tower route (peel `ρ = restrictScalars gsR ∘ restrictScalars appTop σ₁` + `powers_restrictScalars` ×2 + `of_restrictScalars`); (c) if the wall resists a 5th time, the planner's armed pivot — a direct scheme-level `IsQuasicoherent`-of-affine-pushforward criterion. NOT the by-hand `letI Module.compHom`.
4131	
4132	## Knowledge Base — Tooling (iter-239 addition)
4133	
4134	- **Informal agent DOWN since ≥iter-234 — `MOONSHOT_API_KEY` returns HTTP 401 Invalid Authentication (iter-239 re-confirmed).** Both provers tried `archon-informal-agent.py --provider auto` and got 401; no other provider key (`DEEPSEEK/OPENAI/OPENROUTER/GEMINI_API_KEY`) is set. The "second opinion when stuck" affordance is unavailable. Plan around its absence (use mathlib-analogist instead) until the user rotates the key.
4135	
4136	## Knowledge Base — Proof patterns (iter-240 addition)
4137	
4138	- **Pushforward/pullback composition-coherence of the unit comparison via adjunction-mate transport (iter-240, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`, axiom-clean).** To prove the *pullback-side* coherence `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` — `pbu(h≫f) = (pullbackComp h f).inv ≫ (pullback h).map(pbu f) ≫ pbu h` (the blueprint's "genuinely-new ingredient" for `lem:pullback_unit_iso`; Mathlib-absent at the pin) — do NOT attack it directly. First prove the EASY *pushforward-side* `unitToPushforwardObjUnit_comp` sectionwise (`SheafOfModules.Hom.ext → PresheafOfModules.hom_ext → ModuleCat.hom_ext → LinearMap.ext → rfl`; the pushforward unit comparison is sectionwise just the structure-sheaf ring map). Then transport across the adjunction: `apply (pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h≫f)).homEquiv.injective`, rewrite the LHS adjunct to `unitToPushforwardObjUnit (h≫f)` (`pullbackPushforwardAdjunction_homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit`) then to the composite via the pushforward-side lemma; package the mate identity (`conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv`) relating `(pullbackComp h f).inv` ↔ `(pushforwardComp h f).hom`; collapse the composite-adjunction unit with `Adjunction.comp_unit_app` + `homEquiv_naturality_{left,right}` sub-lemmas; slide `pushforwardComp.hom` past via `NatTrans.naturality`. ~87 lines, `{propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}`.
4139	- **`SheafOfModules R` compositions are defeq-but-NOT-syntactic ⇒ use `erw`, never `rw`, for coherence rewrites (iter-240, reusable).** Category compositions over `SheafOfModules R` appear in two defeq forms (`Scheme.Modules.pullback f` vs `SheafOfModules.pullback f.toRingCatSheafHom`), so plain `rw [Category.assoc / Functor.map_comp / <coherence lemma> / <local have>]` **silently fails** ("pattern not found" / "no progress") even when the goal visibly matches. **Use `erw` for every assoc / functoriality / sub-lemma rewrite.** Bridge the `Adjunction.homEquiv_unit ↔ G.map` higher-order match with an explicit `e := Adjunction.homEquiv_unit (adj:=…) (X:=…) (Y:=…) (f:=…)` then `e.symm.trans` (defeq-tolerant `exact`) — never `rw [← Adjunction.homEquiv_unit]` (its `?adj`/`G.map` HO pattern does not fire). Restate `SheafOfModules.…` lemmas in `Scheme.Modules.…` short-namespace form via `have h : … := by exact <lemma>` so later `rw`s match the goal's printed namespace.
4140	- **`algebraize [φ.hom]` is the carrier-wall fix for `restrictScalars`-of-section localizations (iter-240, `FlatBaseChange.lean`).** The recurring `Module.compHom _ φ.hom` `letI` mechanism for putting an `R`-action on `R'`-side sections is DEAD (not consumed by `LinearMap.restrictScalars R`). The right mechanism is `algebraize [φ.hom]` (run AT the goal), which installs honest `Algebra ↑R ↑R'` (= `φ.hom.toAlgebra`) + `IsScalarTower`. Then discharge `IsLocalizedModule (powers a) (σ.restrictScalars R)` via `@IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` with **explicit** instance args — ordinary TC does NOT auto-pick the `Module R` instances on the bare section carriers, so pass `mTop`/`mDa` (= `inferInstanceAs` of the `(restrictScalars φ.hom).obj` bundled instances) and `tTop`/`tDa` (= `IsScalarTower`) positionally; reconcile `powers (φ a) ↔ algebraMapSubmonoid R' (powers a)` with `Algebra.algebraMapSubmonoid_powers`. NOTE: the pushforward/`modulesSpecToSheaf` peeling through `restrictScalars` over *preimage* opens is DEFINITIONAL (`SpecMap_preimage_basicOpen` is `rfl`) — the structural exposures hold by `rfl`.
4141	
4142	## Knowledge Base — Resolved blockers (iter-240 addition)
4143	
4144	- **FlatBaseChange `hloc` 4-recurrence carrier wall BROKEN (iter-240).** `algebraize [φ.hom]` + `@IsLocalizedModule.powers_restrictScalars` (explicit instances) discharges the `hloc` obligation that stalled iters 234/235/236/239. The whole `of_linearEquiv` transport tail of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` now compiles GIVEN the open-naturality square `hsq`. Supersedes the iter-239 "Module-R carrier wall" blocker (route (a)/(b)/(c) were the options; `algebraize` is effectively a cleaner (b)). The decl is NOT yet sorry-free — the residual moved to `hsq` (see new blocker below).
4145	
4146	## Knowledge Base — Known Blockers (iter-240 addition)
4147	
4148	- **`gammaPushforwardIsoAt` open-naturality square (`hsq`) won't rewrite-unify — repackage as a `NatIso` (iter-240, `FlatBaseChange.lean`).** The sole residual sorry of `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` is `hsq : ρ ≫ e₂.hom = e₁.hom ≫ Gmor` (naturality-in-the-open of `gammaPushforwardIsoAt`), a composite of 3 squares. `nat1` (the exact inv-naturality first piece) typechecks, but ~15 distinct rewrite forms (`rw [nat1]`, `reassoc_of% nat1`, `simp [..._inv_naturality_assoc]`, `slice_lhs`, `conv_lhs`, `set X … ; rw [← Category.assoc X, nat1]`) ALL fail to unify the `restrictScalarsComp'App _ _ _ _ _ .inv` subterm against `nat1`'s LHS **despite identical printing** — a representational/universe discrepancy between the `App` baked into `gammaPushforwardIsoAt` (built inside a `by`-block with `set`s) and a freshly-applied `restrictScalarsComp'App`. **DO NOT retry the same rewrite forms.** FIX (ranked): (1) refactor `gammaPushforwardIsoAt` (lives in THIS file; consumers use only `.app U`) into a genuine `NatIso` via `NatIso.ofComponents` → naturality-in-open becomes the `.naturality` field, definitional; (2) Mathlib bump #37189 (`isIso_fromTildeΓ_pushforward`) collapses the def to ~3 lines (the recorded HARD reversing-signal — take it if the NatIso refactor stalls); (3) element-wise (blocked on an `eqToHom`-applied-to-element identity lemma). NOTE: `lean_diagnostic_messages` over a line *range* can be STALE/partial (reported "no errors" for a range excluding the failing `rw`); always confirm with `lake env lean`.
4149	- **`pullbackObjUnitToUnit` instance-synthesis non-canonicity blocks `pullbackUnitIso` assembly (iter-240, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`) — pure plumbing, NOT math.** With the linchpin `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` landed, the per-chart `isIso_restrict_pullbackObjUnitToUnit` recipe is complete and its `▸`/`of_isIso_comp_*`/`choose`/globalizer parts typecheck, BUT inside the rich per-chart context `infer_instance` FAILS to synthesise `IsIso (pbu U.ι)`, `IsIso (pbu g)`, `IsIso ((pullbackComp g U.ι).inv.app _)` — all of which synthesise FINE STANDALONE (verified `lean_run_code`). Cause: `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ` carries non-canonical implicit instance args (`[(pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint]`, `[F.IsContinuous]`); after `rw [pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp]` the produced subterms bind those implicits to forms the in-scope `(Opens.map _).Final` haveIs no longer match. `set`/`clear_value`/`asIso`-`mapIso`/`refine @IsIso.comp_isIso ?_ ?_ ?_` all fail identically. FIX (ranked): (1) build each component iso as a fully type-ascribed named `Iso` BEFORE the `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` rewrite, then `convert`/transport `IsIso` across the coherence eq (pins implicits, no re-synthesis); (2) add `@[instance] lemma isIso_pullbackObjUnitToUnit_of_final` with the canonical implicit shape, apply `@`-explicitly; (3) mathlib-analogist consult. CAVEAT (lean-auditor): the name `instIsIsoPullbackObjUnitToUnitOfFinal` cited in the HANDOFF/blueprint is **absent** from Mathlib and all project `.lean` — verify the real name via `lean_local_search` before the next attempt.
4150	
4151	## Knowledge Base — Resolved blockers (iter-241 addition)
4152	
4153	- **FlatBaseChange `pushforward_spec_tilde_iso` CLOSED axiom-clean — the 4-iter `hsq`/carrier wall is fully resolved (iter-241).** The sole residual (the open-naturality square `hsq : ρ ≫ e₂.hom = e₁.hom ≫ Gmor` of `gammaPushforwardIsoAt`) that stalled iters 237–240 closed by **`ext x; rfl`** after a one-line structural refactor (see Proof patterns below). `lean_verify AlgebraicGeometry.pushforward_spec_tilde_iso = {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (review re-verified first-hand); file sorry 3→2. The iter-240 reversing trip-wire ("flat sorry → Mathlib bump #37189") is SATISFIED by success — **the #37189 bump is NOT needed.** Supersedes the iter-240 `hsq` known-blocker. Still open in the file: `affineBaseChange_pushforward_iso` (own sub-lane, see below) and `flatBaseChange_pushforward_isIso` (deep Čech, out of scope).
4154	- **`pullbackUnitIso` (Phase 1, `lem:pullback_unit_iso`) LANDED axiom-clean — and the blueprint chart-chase is UNNECESSARY (iter-241, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** MAJOR FINDING: `SheafOfModules.pullbackObjUnitToUnit f` is an iso for **every** scheme morphism `f`, not only `Final`-chart ones — `(Opens.map f.base).Final` holds *unconditionally* (preimage-on-opens preserves finite limits ⇒ representably flat ⇒ `CategoryTheory.final_of_representablyFlat`). So `pullbackUnitIso f := pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso f` after `haveI : (Opens.map f.base).Final := final_of_representablyFlat _` — a one-liner. The elaborate affine chart-chase the blueprint demands (and even the iter-240 linchpin `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`) is NOT needed for the unit iso; the prover built the full chart-chase `isIso_restrict_pbu` axiom-clean but did NOT commit it (redundant). `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` is RETAINED (genuine Mathlib-absent pseudofunctor coherence; most plausible Phase-2 ingredient). `lean_verify pullbackUnitIso = {propext, Classical.choice, Quot.sound}` (review re-verified). Phase 2/3 still open (see below). Resolves the iter-240 `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` instance-synthesis known-blocker (the TC accident was real but only a proof-tactic issue — fixed by the `@asIso`-explicit-witness idiom below).
4155	
4156	## Knowledge Base — Proof patterns (iter-241 addition)
4157	
4158	- **Stuck `restrictScalars`-iso naturality square → delete the `eqToIso`, then `ext x; rfl` (iter-241, `FlatBaseChange.lean`, the keystone fix).** When a chain of `restrictScalars`-based isos has a naturality square that resists ALL rewrite forms (`rw`/`simp`/`slice`/`conv`/`reassoc_of%`, ~15 tried over iters 237–240, all failing to unify a `restrictScalarsComp'App … .inv` subterm despite identical printing): the blocker is a single `eqToIso`/`eqToHom` cast breaking definitional equality. **Recipe:** replace the `eqToIso (congrArg (fun h => (restrictScalars h).obj M) hcomp)` with the identity-on-element NatIso component `(ModuleCat.restrictScalarsCongr hcomp).app M` (built from `AddEquiv.refl`, clean `rfl` apply-lemma, SAME type, consumer-safe). All `restrictScalars*` repackaging maps (`restrictScalarsComp'App_{hom,inv}_apply`, `restrictScalarsCongr` app, `restrictScalars.map_apply`) are `rfl`-identity on the underlying carrier (`restrictScalars` never changes the carrier type); once the one `eqToHom` is gone, both legs are defeq and `ext x; rfl` closes the square with no rewrite-matching at all. This is the element-level realization of a "repackage as `NatIso` and use `.naturality`" directive — cheaper than the full `NatIso.ofComponents` refactor.
4159	- **`@asIso … (explicit witness)` to bundle an `Iso` when `asIso`/`infer_instance` fails at reducible transparency (iter-241, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** `pullbackObjUnitToUnit φ` carries buried implicit instance args (`[F.IsContinuous]`, `[(pushforward φ).IsRightAdjoint]`) that are defeq-but-not-syntactic when several `(Opens.map _).Final` are in scope, so `asIso (pbu g)` / `Functor.map_isIso _ _ (pbu g)` FAIL to synthesise `IsIso (pbu g)` (reducible transparency) even with a type-ascribed `haveI` in scope. **Fix (the `Functor.Monoidal.μIso` bundled-iso idiom):** (1) `private lemma isIso_pbu_of_final g [(Opens.map g.base).Final] : IsIso (pbu g) := inferInstance` isolates the lone `Final` so synthesis runs at a clean site; (2) `def pullbackObjUnitToUnitIso g := @asIso _ _ _ _ (pbu g) (isIso_pbu_of_final g)` passes the witness EXPLICITLY — the `@asIso` typecheck is `isDefEq` at DEFAULT transparency, which succeeds where reducible synthesis does not. Asymmetry note: `have t : IsIso (pbu g) := inferInstance` (type-ascribed) WORKS because the expected type pins the implicits before synthesis; `asIso (pbu g)` does not. Always pass instances explicitly through `@asIso`.
4160	
4161	## Knowledge Base — Process gotcha (iter-241 addition)
4162	
4163	- **`sync_leanok` mis-inserts `\leanok` INSIDE a multi-line `\uses{...}` brace when `\uses{` opens immediately after `\begin{proof}` (iter-241, `Cohomology_FlatBaseChange.tex` L500/L551).** When a proof block is `\begin{proof}` then a `\uses{label1, label2,` continued on the next line, `sync_leanok` puts the proof-block `\leanok` on its own line *between* the `\uses{` opener and the rest of the label list, e.g. `\uses{lem:a, lem:b,\n  \leanok\n  lem:c}`. This (a) breaks the trailing `\uses` dependency edges (`lem:c` is read as part of a malformed label `\leanok lem:c`) — surfaced by blueprint-doctor as "broken cross-references" even though the labels DO exist — and (b) mis-places the marker. **Root cause is a single misplaced `\leanok`; fixing its position fixes both doctor findings.** This is NOT the review agent's to edit (`\leanok` = sync domain; `\uses{}` prose = plan-agent domain). MITIGATION for the plan agent: move the `\leanok` to directly follow `\begin{proof}` (its own line, before the `\uses{`), or put the whole `\uses{...}` on one line so the sync's insertion point is unambiguous.
4164	
4165	## Knowledge Base — Known Blockers (iter-241 addition)
4166	
4167	- **Phase 2 `pullbackTensorIso` (`f^*(M⊗N) ≅ f^*M ⊗ f^*N`) is a genuine Mathlib-absent build — no comparison map exists yet (iter-241, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** Confirmed by Mathlib grep: there is NO tensor-pullback comparison and NO `MonoidalCategory (SheafOfModules …)` at the pinned commit (only `PresheafOfModules/Monoidal.lean`; `PullbackFree.lean` ships only `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` (unit) + `pullbackObjFreeIso` (free), no tensor). `Scheme.Modules.pullback f` is an abstract left adjoint with no sectionwise/stalkwise value, so there is no canonical map to even *state* the iso, and the `OfFinal` unit trick (which gave Phase 1 for free) does NOT generalise to tensor. ## Knowledge Base — Known Blockers (iter-256 addition)

- **D3′ `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`: the "MIRROR `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp`" recipe is FALSE — do NOT re-dispatch it (iter-256, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean` L2138).** `pullbackObjUnitToUnit_comp` works ONLY because `pullbackObjUnitToUnit` is *definitionally* an adjunction transpose, so its composition coherence falls out of `(pullbackPushforwardAdjunction (h≫f)).homEquiv.injective` + a `…homEquiv_pullbackObjUnitToUnit` bridge. `pullbackTensorMap` is NOT a transpose — `simp only [pullbackTensorMap]` exposes it as a hand-built 4-fold composite `S1 ≫ a.map δ ≫ S3 ≫ S4`, there is NO `…homEquiv_pullbackTensorMap` bridge, so the mirror's opening leaves an un-evaluable transpose and stalls (reversing signal fired as armed). Genuine route = a 4-square **composition**-coherence (cf. D1′'s 4-square *naturality*): Sq1 `sheafificationCompPullback`-comp (Mathlib-absent project sub-lemma ~40-120 LOC), Sq2 `Functor.OplaxMonoidal.comp_δ` + `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp` + ring-map reconcile (`toRingCatSheafHom (h≫f).hom = φ'_f ≫ whiskerLeft φ'_h`, defeq-not-syntactic via `Opens.map_comp`), Sq3 `sheafifyTensorUnitIso`-comp, Sq4 `pullbackValIso`-comp (Mathlib-absent project sub-lemma). The Lean decl proves the GENERAL composition coherence (any `h,f`); the blueprint `lem:pullback_tensor_map_basechange` states only the base-change-square specialization — **statement realignment + sketch replacement is a blueprint must-fix before the next prover pass** (`% NOTE:` added iter-256). `conjugateEquiv_pullbackComp_inv` is the adjunction-mate bridge for Sq2/Sq4.

## Knowledge Base — Engine de-risk (iter-256 addition)

- **`LineBundleCoherence.lean` site-instance de-risk is POSITIVE — the engine pole can go to a FULL prover lane (iter-256).** All four slice-site instances on `J.over U` (`J = Opens.grothendieckTopology X`) that `SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation`/`QuasicoherentData` demand resolve AUTOMATICALLY: `HasSheafCompose (forget₂ RingCat AddCommGrpCat)` (`hasSheafCompose_of_preservesMulticospan`), `HasWeakSheafify`, `WEqualsLocallyBijective`, `HasSheafify`. The bare fact that `M.over U`/`M.IsFinitePresentation`/`M.IsFiniteType` typecheck already forces them (bound as section variables in `Quasicoherent.lean`). No analogist needed for the site question. **Residual risk** = the `chartPresentation` FINITENESS bridge: `chartPresentation` returns a bare `Presentation`; build it via `Presentation.ofIsIso e.hom` on `SheafOfModules.unit`'s canonical finite presentation so `(chartPresentation …).IsFinite` is auto-inferred (or add a 6th decl). `chart_free_rank_one`'s type is a pointwise restatement of `IsLocallyTrivial M` (proof `exact hM x`) — Mathlib has NO `SheafOfModules`-level rank/flat predicate.

## Knowledge Base — Documentation-rot watch (iter-256 addition)

- **`LineBundleCoherence.lean` is NOT imported into `AlgebraicJacobian.lean` (iter-256) — the ROOT of two marker bugs.** The new engine file is absent from the root import list (explicit, no glob). Consequence: (1) not in the loop build or `blueprint/lean_decls`; (2) `sync_leanok` cannot evaluate the new chapter's decls, leaving a **FALSE proof-block `\leanok`** at `Picard_LineBundleCoherence.tex:187` (chartPresentation proof marked closed over a `sorry` body). PLANNER must add `import AlgebraicJacobian.Picard.LineBundleCoherence` so the next sync corrects it + the doctor `covers` lint clears.
- **`sync_leanok` STRIPPED proof-`\leanok` from 3 closed axiom-clean decls this iter (iter-256, sha `4479ed1c`, +8/−24) — under-mark, NOT a Lean regression.** `homOfLocalCompat`, D1′ `pullbackTensorMap_natural`, D2′ `pullbackTensorMap_unit_isIso` are all sorry-free + axiom-clean (verified) but lack proof-`\leanok`. The −24 net (contrast iters 254/255's +17/+30) is the recurring parallel-race / stale-olean strip returning — likely aggravated by the unimported new file disturbing the build the sync reads. A clean re-sync post-import should restore them.
- **The `\uses{\leanok …}` corruption persists at `Picard_RelPicFunctor.tex:144-146` (iter-256 blueprint-doctor).** A stray `\leanok` sits on its own line INSIDE a multiline `\uses{…}` (between the def list and `thm:relative_pic_quotient_well_defined`), so leanblueprint parses `\leanok\n thm:…` as one unmatched label. The label is fine (`Picard_LineBundlePullback.tex:331`). Durable fix = reflow the `\uses{}` to a single line so the sync-inserted `\leanok` cannot land between the braces.

## Knowledge Base — CONVERGENCE finding (iter-257 addition)

- **The engine's `chartOverIso` and the dual lane's `dual_restrict_iso`/`exists_tensorObj_inverse` reduce to the SAME Mathlib-scale build — `SheafOfModules.overEquivalence` (iter-257). HIGHEST-LEVERAGE next action.** Both need the modules-level lift of `Opens.overEquivalence : Over U ≌ Opens ↥U` to an equivalence of sheaf-of-modules categories `SheafOfModules ((↑U).ringCatSheaf) ≌ SheafOfModules (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)` matching `restrict ↦ over`, `unit ↦ unit`. (1) engine `chartOverIso : M.over U ≅ unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)` (sole sorry; closes the A.2.c deliverable `isFinitePresentation`); (2) dual `sliceDualTransport`/`dual_restrict_iso` (the ⊗-inverse). This is the modules-level shadow of `overSliceSheafEquiv` (which exists only at `Sheaf`-of-`Ab` level, fixed ring — inapplicable since the ring VARIES). ~200–350 LOC, own file; primitives in `informal/chartOverIso.md`. CAVEAT: the dual lane currently builds `sliceDualTransport` *sectionwise* (not via the equivalence) — run a mathlib-analogist pass to decide the shared route BEFORE dispatching a `overEquivalence` build.

## Knowledge Base — Resolved blockers (iter-257 addition)

- **`LineBundleCoherence.lean` engine 5 → 1, four bodies axiom-clean (iter-257, A.2.c).** `exists_trivializing_cover` (`I := X` points, `U x := (hM x).choose`, cover by `iSup = ⊤` via `eq_top_iff` + `TopologicalSpace.Opens.mem_iSup ⟨x, …⟩`); `chart_free_rank_one` (`exact hM x`); new reusable §1b bricks `freeUnitIso`/`unitGenerators`/`unitPresentation` (+`IsFinite`). `chartPresentation`/`isFinitePresentation`/`isFiniteType` complete-modulo-`chartOverIso` (`#print axioms` shows `sorryAx` ONLY via that one iso). The planner's `Presentation.ofIsIso e.hom` recipe was TYPE-INCORRECT (the given `e` is in `SheafOfModules (U:Scheme).ringCatSheaf`, `ofIsIso` needs `SheafOfModules (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)` — different categories) → rebuilt through the explicit `chartOverIso` bridge so the whole engine factors through one iso.
- **Sq2 ring-map reconciliation for the composite pullback is DEFINITIONAL — `rfl` (iter-257, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** `toRingCatSheafHom_comp_hom_reconcile` (`(toRingCatSheafHom (h≫f)).hom = (toRingCatSheafHom f).hom ≫ (Opens.map f.base).op.whiskerLeft (toRingCatSheafHom h).hom`) closes by `rfl`; the Scheme composition defeqs hold at default transparency. CORRECTS the iter-256 blueprint claim ("non-trivial, transported by pseudofunctor coherence via `pullback_assoc`/`pullback_comp_id` atoms") — none of that is needed for this sub-step. So `PresheafOfModules.pullbackComp φ'_f φ'_h` lands in `pullback φ'_{h≫f}` on the nose.

## Knowledge Base — Proof patterns (iter-257 addition)

- **`IsFinitePresentation` of a `SheafOfModules` — the universe recipe (iter-257, `LineBundleCoherence.lean`).** `SheafOfModules.IsFinitePresentation`'s existential indexes `QuasicoherentData` at the **site-object universe** (confirm via `#print … with pp.universes`). So: build `let q : M.QuasicoherentData := {…}` (with `coversTop` proved inline: `intro W x hxW; … mem_iSup; exact ⟨U i ⊓ W, homOfLE inf_le_right, ⟨i, ⟨homOfLE inf_le_left⟩⟩, hi, hxW⟩`), then `q.shrink` to land the index at the right universe, then `have hsh : q.shrink.IsFinitePresentation := by apply …IsFinitePresentation.mk; intro i; exact inferInstanceAs (chartPresentation …).IsFinite; exact { exists_quasicoherentData := ⟨q.shrink, hsh⟩ }`. TRAPS: `apply …mk` (NOT `refine ⟨…⟩`, which leaves `Category.{0,0} ?m` stuck); `inferInstanceAs (chartPresentation …).IsFinite` (NOT bare `infer_instance`) to force the defeq through `shrink.presentation`; nested/type-ascribed `⟨…⟩` all default universes to 0.
- **`unit R` has a canonical finite presentation (iter-257, reusable, any `R : Sheaf J RingCat` with the three site instances).** `freeUnitIso : free PUnit ≅ unit R` via `Limits.coproductUniqueIso`; `unitPresentation` = one generator, `relations.I := PEmpty` (NO relations), epi from `IsZero (kernel π)` (π iso ⇒ `Mono` ⇒ `Limits.isZero_kernel_of_mono`) via `⟨fun g h _ => hz.eq_of_src g h⟩`. GOTCHA: precede `isZero_kernel_of_mono` with `have : Mono π := inferInstance` (else stuck `Mono ?m`).

## Knowledge Base — Known Blockers (iter-257 addition)

- **D3′ `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`: the reconcile is done (rfl); the REAL Sq2 is "Sq2b" = monoidality of `pullbackComp`, Mathlib-ABSENT — do NOT re-dispatch a prover round; needs a cross-domain analogist first (iter-257, `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`).** Sq2b = "δ of `pullback φ'_{h≫f}` (leftAdjoint-oplax of the composite adjunction) transports, through `pullbackComp`, to δ of the composite functor `pullback φ'_f ⋙ pullback φ'_h` via `Functor.OplaxMonoidal.comp_δ`". No Mathlib lemma exists (`Adjunction.leftAdjointOplaxMonoidal_δ` / a `leftAdjointCompIso` δ-transport are ABSENT); it must be proved by mate calculus mirroring `CategoryTheory.Adjunction.isMonoidal_comp` (Mathlib `Monoidal/Functor.lean:990`, whose `leftAdjoint_μ` proof uses `comp_δ` + `δ_natural` + `tensorHom_comp_tensorHom`). THREE statement-level frictions block even stating Sq2b: (1) `δ (pullback φ')` leaves `MonoidalCategory` metavars stuck → needs the D1′ CommRingCat/`forget₂` canonical-spelling pin (`show … from` / `let φ' : … ⋙ forget₂`); (2) unifying δ against `pullbackComp`'s codomain needs explicit `(F := _ ⋙ _)` + an associativity defeq (HO stall); (3) the reconcile defeq does NOT fire at the `.app` connecting-object even under `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` → needs an explicit `eqToHom` bridge from `toRingCatSheafHom_comp_hom_reconcile`. Sq1/Sq4 (`sheafificationCompPullback`/`pullbackValIso` comp coherences) are also Mathlib-absent `leftAdjointUniq`-across-composite args, deferred behind Sq2b. pc257 read this lane CONVERGING; the iter's only "close" was the trivial rfl helper while the genuine content is Mathlib-scale — flag for the next progress-critic.

## Knowledge Base — Process gotcha (iter-257 addition)

- **Do NOT co-schedule a prover on a `.lean` file and a prover on a file that IMPORTS it (iter-257).** `DualInverse.lean` imports `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`; running the TS-inv and TS-cmp provers concurrently left the import non-compiling for most of the session, so the TS-inv LSP returned empty goals (`failed_dependencies` whenever the dep source ≠ its `.olean`), making iterative development of the ~200-LOC `sliceDualTransport` body impractical (the prover cited this as decisive in its non-close). When both files have live prover work, SERIALIZE them (or freeze the importee's signature for the iter).
- **The `\uses{\leanok …}` corruption at `Picard_RelPicFunctor.tex:144-146` is RE-INTRODUCED BY `sync_leanok` ITSELF each iter (iter-257).** This iter's sync (sha `a60632e1`, +1/−0) inserted a proof-block `\leanok` on its own line INSIDE the multiline `\uses{…}` argument (opened L144, label list continues L146), splitting the cross-ref list → blueprint-doctor reports the trailing label as a broken ref. The planner's plan-phase manual fix is REVERTED by sync every iter, so a plan-side prose reflow is not durable — this needs a fix to the `sync_leanok` SCRIPT (maintainer) so the proof-block `\leanok` lands directly after `\begin{proof}`, never between `\uses{` braces. NOT the review agent's domain (`\leanok` ownership).

input.content:
# Iter-257 (Archon canonical) — review

## Outcome at a glance

- **The "engine lane crashes 5 → 1 (four bodies axiom-clean) and reveals its sole blocker is the SAME
  over↔restrict wall as the dual lane; both substrate lanes (D3′, dual Step-4) make real structural
  progress but neither closes its target, and both expose that their blueprint sketches are wrong /
  under-specified at the genuine hard step" iter.** Three prover lanes, all `opus`, mode `prove`.
  - **Lane engine** (`Picard/LineBundleCoherence.lean`): **5 → 1**. Closed axiom-clean:
    `exists_trivializing_cover`, `chart_free_rank_one`, + reusable §1b bricks
    `freeUnitIso`/`unitGenerators`/`unitPresentation`. `chartPresentation`/`isFinitePresentation`/
    `isFiniteType` complete-modulo the single iso `chartOverIso` (`#print axioms` shows `sorryAx` only
    via it). The planner's `Presentation.ofIsIso e.hom` recipe was type-incorrect (slice-site vs
    open-subscheme category mismatch); the prover rebuilt the chart presentation through an explicit
    bridge so the engine factors through one iso. **Genuine frontier win.**
  - **Lane TS-cmp** (`Picard/TensorObjSubstrate.lean`, D3′ `pullbackTensorMap_restrict`): **2 → 2**,
    PARTIAL. Closed `toRingCatSheafHom_comp_hom_reconcile` (`rfl`, axiom-clean) and found the Sq2
    ring-map reconcile is DEFINITIONAL (disproving the blueprint's "non-trivial transport" prose). Did
    NOT meet the pc257 bar — the genuine Sq2 (Sq2b = `pullbackComp` monoidality) is Mathlib-absent with
    three documented frictions. Prover correctly removed non-elaborating helpers, kept file green, left a
    typed sorry + ROADMAP.
  - **Lane TS-inv** (`Picard/TensorObjSubstrate/DualInverse.lean`, `dual_restrict_iso` Step-4):
    **1 → 2** (decomposed). New signature-verified helper `sliceDualTransport` (body sorry) + wired
    Step-4 assembly. Not closed: magnitude (~200 LOC) + a decisive cross-lane compilation race.
- **Builds:** all three files green (prover-verified `lake build` exit 0).
- **Canonical critical-path counter:** the ⊗-inverse `exists_tensorObj_inverse` is still open; but the
  **A.2.c engine deliverable** (`isFinitePresentation`) is now one iso from done — a real frontier
  down-move (4 declaration-sorries eliminated).
- **`sync_leanok`** sha `a60632e1`, **+1 / −0** (`Picard_RelPicFunctor.tex`) — and that +1 IS the
  recurring `\uses{}`-corruption (see Structural).

## The defining finding — the engine and the dual lane are the same wall

iter-256 opened the engine lane; iter-257 takes it from a 5-sorry skeleton to a single isolated
isomorphism, with four bodies closed axiom-clean. But the most consequential output is not the count: it
is that the engine's sole remaining sorry (`chartOverIso : M.over U ≅ unit (X.ringCatSheaf.over U)`) is
**the same Mathlib-scale construction** as the dual lane's open chain (`sliceDualTransport` →
`dual_restrict_iso` → `exists_tensorObj_inverse`). Both are the modules-level lift of
`Opens.overEquivalence` (`SheafOfModules.overEquivalence`) — the slice-site ↔ open-subscheme-site
equivalence with a varying ring. The two highest-priority open obligations on the Picard critical path
now share one root build (~200–350 LOC, own file; `informal/chartOverIso.md`). This is the single
clearest planning signal of the iter: **build `SheafOfModules.overEquivalence` once, unblock both** —
after a mathlib-analogist pass reconciles the routes (the dual prover currently builds `sliceDualTransport`
sectionwise, not via the equivalence).

## The honest counterweight — two CONVERGING lanes that did not converge

pc257 read both substrate lanes CONVERGING. The iter's evidence pushes back on that:

1. **D3′ "converged" on a triviality.** The only thing TS-cmp closed (`toRingCatSheafHom_comp_hom_reconcile`)
   turned out to be `rfl` — the blueprint had mislabelled a definitional fact as "non-trivial transport".
   The genuine content (Sq2b, `pullbackComp` monoidality) is now exposed as Mathlib-absent, mirroring
   `Adjunction.isMonoidal_comp` by mate calculus, with three statement-level frictions that block even
   *stating* it. That is closer to STUCK-risk than CONVERGING, and the next progress-critic should be told.
   The correct next move is a cross-domain analogist consult BEFORE any prover round — the pattern that
   paid off twice (whisker252, mapin255, dualstep4-257).
2. **Dual Step-4 was throttled by a process error, not just magnitude.** The prover's non-close is partly
   the genuine ~200-LOC build, but it explicitly cites the cross-lane compilation race as decisive:
   `DualInverse.lean` imports `TensorObjSubstrate.lean`, which the concurrent TS-cmp prover broke for most
   of the session, so the LSP returned empty goals. This is a dispatch-design bug — do not co-schedule a
   prover on a file and a prover on its importer.

Neither lane closed its assigned target, and all three prover-touched chapters now carry must-fix
blueprint findings (below). The forward motion is real (engine 4-close; both substrate lanes have
verified structural floors), but the next iter cannot simply re-dispatch.

## Subagent verdicts (this review)

- **lean-auditor `aud257`:** files honest — all sorries correctly typed, no vacuous bodies, no
  excuse-comments. 5 MAJOR **stale comments** (file headers undercount sorries; a stale ROADMAP
  "ITER-257 FINDINGS" describing already-done `rfl` work; a stale comment inside the *closed*
  `homOfLocalCompat` at L639; a borderline-excuse "concurrently-broken dependency" note). All prover-fixable.
- **lvb-lbc257 / lvb-tos257 / lvb-dual257:** all three return **must-fix BLUEPRINT** findings —
  `Picard_LineBundleCoherence.tex` elides the `chartOverIso` bridge; `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` Sq2
  prose is false + Sq2b absent (D3′) and the dual Step-4 assembly is stale. The TensorObjSubstrate chapter
  `% archon:covers` both substrate files, so one writer pass fixes the gate for both lanes.

## Markers / structural (details in summary.md)

- Added two `% NOTE:` annotations (review domain) flagging the disproven/under-specified sketches in
  `Picard_LineBundleCoherence.tex` (chartOverIso bridge) and `Picard_TensorObjSubstrate.tex` (Sq2 false /
  Sq2b absent). No `\mathlibok`, no `\lean{}` renames, no stale `\notready`.
- blueprint-doctor: `Cohomology_CechHigherDirectImage.tex` (new) covers a non-existent `.lean` + 5 broken
  `\ref`; `Picard_RelPicFunctor.tex:144-146` `\uses{}`-corruption re-introduced by `sync_leanok` itself.

## Subagent skips

(none — all review-phase recommended subagents dispatched: lean-auditor + per-file lean-vs-blueprint
for all 3 prover-touched files.)
